description:
Goal
Delete the remaining places where `any` or `none` encode uncertainty, missing evidence, stale broad evidence, or incomplete convergence instead of real source/spec/runtime semantics. After activation partitioning and ReturnEvidence, every remaining top/bottom type in semantic output must be justified by the language model, not by scheduler readiness.

Signal
- Every `any_ty(world)` and `none_ty(world)` in compiler2 semantic/type flow is classified in tests or code structure as domain-top/domain-bottom, dynamic-boundary erasure, or deleted poison.
- Protocol, callable, map/field, receive, pinned comparison, function contract, extern/provider-boundary, and closure-call paths no longer return `any` just because a producer module/function/fact was missing.
- Empty value sets (`none`) are produced only by impossible input evidence or true no-return/divergence, not by absent callee return evidence.
- Tests that formerly passed because unknown widened to `any` now pass through explicit waits, evidence states, or diagnostics.
- Telemetry shows evidence movement and partition convergence, not broad `any`/`none` facts freezing the frontier.

Strategy
- Start with a grep/audit table over the semantic/type fallback sites, then turn each disputed fallback into an intent-named test before changing behavior.
- For each fallback, trace backward to the earliest fact that should have expressed missing/unknown/known evidence and fix that fact shape or wait condition.
- Use `ReturnEvidence` for return unknowns, settled waits for completeness requirements, and explicit dynamic-erasure helpers only at real runtime/external boundaries.
- Prefer deleting helper branches over preserving compatibility names; if three source spellings are the same semantic case, keep one test with source comments rather than triplicate accidental fixtures.
- Do not add display/planner/backend patches that merely hide a poisoned type downstream.

Outro
No missing-return-as-`none`, no unresolved-as-`any`, no underconstrained-call-as-`any` without an explicit dynamic boundary, no local fallback shim around protocol/runtime loading, no test that blesses broad top/bottom because it was convenient, and no unclassified semantic `any_ty`/`none_ty` fallback in touched compiler2 paths.
title: Compiler2 type convergence: burn down any/none poison fallbacks
